userMessage: add some more statistics if you check scope screen you can see the options enable histrogram for meas 3 frequency

response.text:
Done — The display shows an eye/jitter analysis of Ref1 for a ~2.50 Gb/s serial signal. The main eye diagram is open but narrowed by jitter and intersymbol interference, with midlevel eye height about 80.36 mV and eye width about 345.9 ps. The Measurement Results table for Meas 1 lists jitter summary values including data rate 2.5008 Gb/s, PL(UI)=40, TIE(σ) about -39 zs mean with minimum about -31.882 ps shown, total jitter about 54.07 ps, and RJ about 1.6804 ps, indicating deterministic jitter is the dominant impairment.

The lower-left plots show a TIE histogram with a clustered, asymmetric distribution and a TIE spectrum with several discrete spur components, reinforcing that the jitter is not purely random. The bathtub plot is visible and used to estimate BER margin versus sampling position.

The waveform view below the eye shows the underlying signal over a long time span with amplitude centered near 0 V and swing roughly from about -550 mV to +500 mV. On the right measurement badges, Meas 2 is Period with mean 1.066 ns. Meas 3 is Frequency and now has statistics enabled: mean 1.106 GHz, sigma 298.4 MHz, minimum 1.266 GHz, maximum 359.0 MHz, and N=1874 samples visible on the badge. Meas 4 is Amplitude with mean 744.8 mV, Meas 5 is Peak-to-Peak with mean 1.104 V, Meas 6 is Rise Time with mean 145.4 ps, and Meas 7 is Fall Time. Overall, the link is operating, but its eye closure is mainly driven by deterministic/data-dependent effects rather than random noise.
